title: OpenAI publishes a Codex Security plugin quickstart
friendlyTitle: OpenAI explains how to run a Codex security scan
summary: OpenAI’s developer documentation now walks users through installing the Codex Security plugin and running a first read-only scan of a local repository inside a Codex thread. Completed scans produce a findings workspace plus portable artifacts such as reports, manifests, findings, and coverage data.
friendlySummary: OpenAI published instructions for using a Codex plugin that scans code for possible security problems and shows evidence and fixes.
whyItMatters: AI code generation is only useful if review and remediation keep pace. A local, reviewable security workflow gives developers a practical pattern: scan authorized code, inspect evidence, export findings, and keep a human in the approval loop before changes ship.
friendlyWhyItMatters: If AI helps write code, it should also help check code. This gives developers a clearer way to review security before releasing software.
